mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and request batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.19.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM instance with 2 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify vLLM installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls vLLM from the 'vllm' namespace.
> This command deletes the 'vllm' namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version. After the upgrade, verify that the application is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o json > vllm-backup.json

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command backs up the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.19.0.
> This command verifies that the vLLM instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o json > vllm-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM controller using 'kubectl logs -l app=vllm -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the vLLM service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service might not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' to check the pod status and 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has sufficient CPU and memory resources available for vLLM.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for vLLM may not have been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to install the CRDs: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old model configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 0.18.0, update your model configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' showing the status of your vLLM instance as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
